shared_facts: The European Union has issued statements regarding Israeli settlement expansion in the West Bank.
Violence and military actions are occurring within the West Bank and Gaza Strip involving Israeli forces and settlers.

Demonstrators in London are demanding that the incoming British government impose an arms embargo on Israel amid continued reports of airstrikes in Gaza. Sources state that these events take place against a backdrop of failing ceasefire agreements and rising death tolls.
latest_clusters.confidence: HIGH; The sources consistently report on events from a singular editorial perspective, leading to high factual consistency within the provided narrative.

shared_facts: Sonam Wangchuk engaged in a hunger strike for approximately 20 days.
Wangchuk was removed from a protest site in New Delhi by authorities and taken to a hospital.
The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) is a protest movement involved in the demonstrations.
Abhijeet Dipke is identified as the founder of the Cockroach Janta Party.
latest_clusters.synthesis: Activists associated with the Cockroach Janta Party are conducting protests in New Delhi, including hunger strikes, to advocate for changes in India's education and examination systems. Following a 20-day hunger strike, activist Sonam Wangchuk was removed from his protest site by police and taken to a hospital. In response to his removal, CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke has initiated an indefinite fast to continue the movement's activities.
latest_clusters.confidence: HIGH. The core events regarding the hunger strike, the activist's removal, and the involvement of the Cockroach Janta Party are consistently reported by both sources.

shared_facts: Jens Spahn has resigned from his position as a parliamentary group leader of the CDU/CSU.
Jens Spahn and his husband, Daniel Funke, used a surrogate mother in the United States to have a child.
Surrogacy is currently banned within Germany.
Jens Spahn was previously critical of surrogacy practices.
latest_clusters.synthesis: Jens Spahn has resigned from his parliamentary leadership role after it was revealed he and his husband used a surrogate in the United States to become parents. The practice of surrogacy remains illegal in Germany and conflicts with the past public statements made by Spahn and the official position of his party. His departure follows internal party pressure regarding the perceived inconsistency between his personal actions and his political record.
latest_clusters.confidence: MEDIUM. There is a factual discrepancy regarding the exact title of the role from which the subject resigned, indicating inconsistent reporting across sources.
